name: Honduras
avoid: May–November is wetter across both mainland and Bay Islands, with rougher ferries, muddier national-park trails, and the highest hurricane disruption risk from late summer into autumn.
Semana Santa and Christmas/New Year weeks push up island hotel rates fast, especially in West Bay, West End, and Utila's dive hostels.
Long mainland transfers are best done by day on reputable operators such as Hedman Alas; avoid stacking late bus arrivals with same-evening ferry departures from La Ceiba.
stopoverNote: Tegucigalpa works best as a quick air hub, while La Ceiba is the key logistics bridge for Bay Islands ferries and Easy Sky domestic hops.

cities.name: Tegucigalpa
cities.notes: Capital arrival base for museums, hilltop viewpoints, Suyapa basilica visits, and onward flights or buses to the north coast and Copán corridor.

cities.name: Copán Ruins
cities.notes: Western Honduras highlight for the UNESCO Maya site, sculpture museum, bird park, and a slower cobblestoned small-town stay.

cities.name: La Ceiba
cities.notes: North-coast gateway for Pico Bonito National Park, the Cangrejal River, ferry docks to Roatán and Utila, and domestic flights with Easy Sky.

cities.name: Roatán
cities.notes: Bay Islands classic for West Bay beach time, easy reef access, sloth sanctuaries, and some of the country's best beginner-friendly dive operations.

cities.name: Utila
cities.notes: Compact backpacker-favorite island with cheaper dive culture, whale shark lookouts in season, and a lively waterfront built around the municipal cays and reef boats.

itinerary.sim: Tigo or Claro prepaid SIM/eSIM is the easiest setup on arrival; budget around ₹850–₹1,700 for a short-stay data pack. Coverage is fine in Tegucigalpa and Copán town, but weaker on Pico Bonito trails and on ferry crossings, so download offline maps before island transfers.

Tegucigalpa.name: Tegucigalpa
Tegucigalpa.note: Keep Tegucigalpa short and practical: stay around Multiplaza, Palmira, or the airport corridor, then move on early before traffic thickens.

Copán Ruins.name: Copán Ruins
Copán Ruins.note: Stay in town and walk almost everywhere; the archaeological park, sculpture museum road, and cafes are easiest when you avoid midday heat.

activities.name: Parque Arqueológico Copán foreigner entrance ticket
activities.tip: Recent public pricing is about 400 HNL for nonresidents; bring small cash in case card machines are unreliable.

activities.name: Museo de Escultura Maya
Los Sapos and Sepulturas sector add-on

La Ceiba.name: La Ceiba
La Ceiba.note: Most travelers use La Ceiba as both a rainforest break and a ferry logistics stop; sleep near the pier or at Pico Bonito depending on your next morning plan.

activities.name: Parque Nacional Pico Bonito visitor entrance
activities.tip: Recent public quotes place simple park access at roughly 200 HNL, while guided walks from lodges or operators cost extra.

activities.name: El Bejuco or Zacate waterfall trail in the Pico Bonito buffer zone
Cangrejal River viewpoint or rafting base stop
Paseo de los Ceibeños waterfront stroll

activities.name: Cuero y Salado Wildlife Refuge motorized canoe excursion
Muelle de Cabotaje ferry check-in for Bay Islands
Mall Megaplaza supply run before island transfer

Roatán.name: Roatán
Roatán.note: West End suits divers and nightlife, while West Bay works better for sandy-beach stays; taxis between the two are short but expensive by local standards.

days.theme: West Bay Beach, Wildlife Parks & Easy Reef Time

activities.name: West Bay Beach swim and snorkel from shore
Gumbalimba Park day pass
Daniel Johnson's Monkey & Sloth Hang Out
West End sunset boardwalk and Half Moon Bay stop
Sun Divers Roatan or West End Divers 2-tank boat dive
activities.tip: Recent public rates for a standard 2-tank fun dive sit around US$74–80 with tanks and weights included; marine-park fee may be separate.

activities.name: West End house-reef snorkel if not diving
Maya Key or Little French Key beach-club half day
Craft market and beachfront cafés around West Bay

hotels.name: Kimpton Grand Roatan Resort and Spa

Utila.name: Utila
Utila.note: Utila is compact enough to do with one or two nights; book whale shark lookouts flexibly because sightings depend on weather, season, and captain calls.

activities.name: Utila Dive Center or WSORC whale shark lookout / snorkel trip
activities.tip: Recent public pricing for seasonal lookout trips is roughly US$45–65 depending on boat, fuel, and sighting conditions.

activities.name: Bando Beach or Pumpkin Hill viewpoint
Chepes Beach quick dip or kayak rental
Municipal dock sunset and dive-town café crawl

days.theme: Cheap Fun Dives, Cays & Lazy Island Time

Utila Cays boat ride

connections.method: Hedman Alas or other long-distance coach via San Pedro Sula; private transfer is faster but much pricier
Day coach back through San Pedro Sula, commonly mixing Hedman Alas with onward north-coast service
Galaxy Wave ferry from Muelle de Cabotaje to Coxen Hole
Return by ferry or short flight to the mainland, then connect onward on the Utila Dream ferry from La Ceiba
Utila Dream ferry back to La Ceiba plus Easy Sky domestic flight onward to Tegucigalpa

extras: December to April is the cleanest all-round window: drier mainland roads, calmer Bay Islands crossings, and the best odds of clear reef days.
Galaxy Wave to Roatán and Utila Dream to Utila can both be choppy in windier weather, so keep one buffer half-day if you are connecting to an international flight.
Carry cash for Copán ticket booths, small-town taxis, baggage tips, and some island dive shops even though bigger hotels and restaurants take cards.
Easy Sky schedules can shift seasonally, so confirm baggage rules and reconfirm your domestic sector the evening before if you are flying out of La Ceiba or Tegucigalpa.
Copán Ruinas rewards an early start, while Roatán and Utila are best when you lock in dive or whale shark boats the day before and stay flexible around sea conditions.
